title: atriplex polycarpa
intro: Atriplex polycarpa, also known as the big sagebrush cholla or big cholla, is a shrub-like plant species in the Amaranthaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Atriplex polycarpa include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Atriplex polycarpa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
